skill.name: dev-servers
skill.description: Start local FastAPI and Vite development servers with dependency checks, setup guidance, and secret-handling guardrails.
skill.summary: Run backend and frontend development servers for a local web application.
skill.author: AppleLamps

security_audit.summary: Static analysis found command execution, localhost URLs, and environment key references. Review confirms the skill is a local development-server helper, not a confirmed exfiltration or prompt-injection attempt, but it does ask an agent to install dependencies and run Bash commands.

medium_findings.title: Dependency Installation Commands
medium_findings.description: The skill instructs the agent to run pip install and npm install when dependencies are missing. This is expected for local setup, but it can execute third-party package install scripts from project files.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The commands are explicit setup instructions and would run through Bash. The risk is real but tied to normal developer workflow rather than malicious intent.

medium_findings.title: Local Development Server Execution
medium_findings.description: The skill instructs the agent to start uvicorn and Vite development servers. This opens local ports and runs project code, which is legitimate but requires user awareness.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The server commands and localhost ports are explicitly documented. They are local development actions, so the security concern is operational exposure, not confirmed abuse.

medium_findings.title: Environment Secret Presence Check
medium_findings.description: The skill asks the agent to verify that backend environment configuration contains an OPENAI_API_KEY. It also instructs the agent not to expose or log the key.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file references a sensitive key by name and checks for its presence. The guardrail reduces risk, and I found no evidence that the key is printed or transmitted.

low_findings.title: False Positive Ruby Backtick Detection
low_findings.description: Several static findings label Markdown inline code as Ruby or shell backtick execution. Lines 11, 12, and 22 describe paths or environment names, not executable Ruby code.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited text is Markdown instruction content. There is no Ruby file, Ruby interpreter invocation, or dynamic backtick execution at these locations.

low_findings.title: Localhost URL Network Finding
low_findings.description: The hardcoded URLs are localhost ports for FastAPI and Vite development servers. I found no evidence of calls to external domains or data exfiltration.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Both URLs point to localhost and document expected local ports. This is not evidence of outbound network transfer.

low_findings.title: False Positive Weak Cryptography Detection
low_findings.description: The static weak-cryptography finding points to the description line. I found no cryptographic algorithm, hashing function, or encryption logic in that line.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line 3 only describes FastAPI and Vite server startup. No evidence supports a weak cryptographic algorithm finding.

low_findings.title: Dismissed Dangerous Combination Heuristic
low_findings.description: The combined code execution, network, and credential heuristic is not confirmed as malicious. The commands are local setup steps, the URLs are localhost, and the key guidance includes a no-disclosure guardrail.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The risky primitives are present, but the semantic context is ordinary local development setup. I found no prompt injection, hidden override text, or external exfiltration endpoint.

dangerous_patterns.title: Bash Command Execution
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill allows Bash and includes package installation plus server startup commands. Users should review project dependency files and confirm installs before execution.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: Allowed-tools explicitly includes Bash, and the skill lists concrete shell commands. The pattern is intentional and visible.

content.value_statement: Starting backend and frontend servers often requires repeated setup checks. This skill guides Claude, Codex, or Claude Code through local FastAPI and Vite startup steps.
actual_capabilities: Checks whether backend and frontend dependency folders are present.
Guides backend dependency installation from requirements.txt.
Guides frontend dependency installation with npm install.
Starts a FastAPI backend with uvicorn reload mode.
Starts a Vite frontend development server.
Verifies that an OpenAI API key is configured without logging it.
limitations: Assumes the project uses backend and frontend directories.
Assumes the backend entry point is main:app.
Does not manage production deployment or process supervision.
Requires user confirmation before installing missing dependencies.

use_cases.title: Run a Full-Stack App Locally
use_cases.description: Start both FastAPI and Vite servers for a local development session.

use_cases.title: Onboard a New Contributor
use_cases.description: Check dependencies, confirm environment setup, and launch the app with clear local ports.

use_cases.title: Validate Local Environment Setup
use_cases.description: Confirm dependency folders and required secret configuration before running the project.

prompt_templates.title: Start the App
prompt_templates.prompt: Start the backend and frontend development servers for this project.

prompt_templates.title: Check Setup First
prompt_templates.prompt: Check whether the backend and frontend dependencies are installed, then tell me what is missing before starting servers.

prompt_templates.title: Launch With Port Checks
prompt_templates.scenario: Avoid conflicts with existing local services
prompt_templates.prompt: Before launching the app, check whether ports 8000 and 5173 are already in use.

prompt_templates.title: Recover a Broken Local Run
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced troubleshooting across backend and frontend
prompt_templates.prompt: Diagnose why the local FastAPI and Vite servers are not starting, then propose the smallest safe fix.

output_examples.output: The assistant checks dependencies, confirms required setup, and starts the backend on port 8000 and frontend on port 5173.

output_examples.input: Run the app locally, but do not expose my API key.
output_examples.output: The assistant verifies that the key exists, avoids printing it, and continues with local server startup.

output_examples.input: The frontend port is already busy.
output_examples.output: The assistant warns about the port conflict and asks whether to stop the existing process or use another port.

best_practices: Review dependency files before allowing package installation.
Keep API keys in environment files and never paste them into chat.
Stop unused local servers when the development session ends.
anti_patterns: Do not use this skill for production deployment.
Do not run dependency installation without user confirmation.
Do not print environment file contents to verify secret values.

faq.question: What does this skill start?
faq.answer: It starts a FastAPI backend with uvicorn and a Vite frontend development server.

faq.question: Does it install dependencies automatically?
faq.answer: It instructs the assistant to confirm with the user before installing missing backend or frontend dependencies.

faq.question: Which ports does it use?
faq.answer: The documented defaults are port 8000 for the backend and port 5173 for the frontend.

faq.question: Does it read my OpenAI API key?
faq.answer: It checks that the key is configured, but it also says never to expose or log the key.

faq.question: Can it work with other frameworks?
faq.answer: It is written for a backend directory using FastAPI and a frontend directory using Vite.

faq.question: Is this safe to publish?
faq.answer: Yes, with a medium-risk warning because it runs Bash commands and references environment secrets.
